title: Cannot create container with bridge network in rootless mode
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When trying to create a container using a network created with the `podman network create` command, an error is raised by `rootless-cni-infra`:

```
Error: command rootless-cni-infra [alloc 9e7e17a1f2a9bc6738b43f0c30f5e04cf3a709379d5e264a3dc0666d1a2e2655 foo dreamy_visvesvaraya   ] in container d2dfe0a386119fb0f42ac3ce16479a5dad0b71086ddd15994bf4876a44014b63 failed with status 1, stdout="", stderr="exit status 3\n"
```
By editing the `rootless-cni-infra` script  with `set -x` I managed to see that `Exit status 3` is printed by `cnitool add foo /proc/19293/ns/net`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
$ podman network create foo
/home/michele/.config/cni/net.d/foo.conflist

$ podman run --rm -it --network=foo alpine:latest ip addr
Error: command rootless-cni-infra [alloc 9e7e17a1f2a9bc6738b43f0c30f5e04cf3a709379d5e264a3dc0666d1a2e2655 foo dreamy_visvesvaraya   ] in container d2dfe0a386119fb0f42ac3ce16479a5dad0b71086ddd15994bf4876a44014b63 failed with status 1, stdout="", stderr="exit status 3\n"
```

**Describe the results you received:**

I couldn't create the container

**Describe the results you expected:**

Being able to create the container

**Additional information**

This is how the output looks like after adding `set -x` to the `rootless-cni-infra` script inside the running `rootless-cni-infra` container, and then trying to create another container:

```
$ podman run --rm -it --network=foo alpine:latest ip addr
Error: command rootless-cni-infra [alloc 2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a foo wonderful_edison   ] in container d2dfe0a386119fb0f42ac3ce16479a5dad0b71086ddd15994bf4876a44014b63 failed with status 1, stdout="", stderr="+ ARG0=/usr/local/bin/rootless-cni-infra\n+ BASE=/run/rootless-cni-infra\n+ command=alloc\n+ '[' -z alloc ]\n+ echo cmd_entrypoint_alloc\n+ sed -e s/-/_/g\n+ command_func=cmd_entrypoint_alloc\n+ command -v cmd_entrypoint_alloc\n+ shift\n+ cmd_entrypoint_alloc 2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a foo wonderful_edison   \n+ '[' 6 -ne 6 ]\n+ ID=2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a\n+ NET=foo\n+ K8S_POD_NAME=wonderful_edison\n+ IP=\n+ MAC=\n+ CAP_ARGS=\n+ dir=/run/rootless-cni-infra/2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a\n+ mkdir -p /run/rootless-cni-infra/2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a/attached /run/rootless-cni-infra/2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a/attached-args\n+ pid=\n+ '[' -f /run/rootless-cni-infra/2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a/pid ]\n+ pid=19293\n+ wait_unshare_net 19293\n+ pid=19293\n+ i=0\n+ :\n+ unshare -n sleep infinity\n+ readlink /proc/self/ns/net\n+ readlink /proc/19293/ns/net\n+ '[' 'net:[4026532484]' '!=' 'net:[4026533434]' ]\n+ break\n+ echo 19293\n+ nsenter -t 19293 -n ip link set lo up\n+ CNI_ARGS='IgnoreUnknown=1;K8S_POD_NAME=wonderful_edison'\n+ '['  ]\n+ '['  ]\n+ '['  ]\n+ find /run/rootless-cni-infra/2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a/attached -type f\n+ wc -l\n+ nwcount=0\n+ CNI_IFNAME=eth0\n+ export CNI_ARGS CNI_IFNAME CAP_ARGS\n+ cnitool add foo /proc/19293/ns/net\nexit status 3\n"
```
The stderr content is:

```
+ ARG0=/usr/local/bin/rootless-cni-infra
+ BASE=/run/rootless-cni-infra
+ command=alloc
+ '[' -z alloc ]
+ echo cmd_entrypoint_alloc
+ sed -e s/-/_/g
+ command_func=cmd_entrypoint_alloc
+ command -v cmd_entrypoint_alloc
+ shift
+ cmd_entrypoint_alloc 2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a foo wonderful_edison   
+ '[' 6 -ne 6 ]
+ ID=2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a
+ NET=foo
+ K8S_POD_NAME=wonderful_edison
+ IP=
+ MAC=
+ CAP_ARGS=
+ dir=/run/rootless-cni-infra/2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a
+ mkdir -p /run/rootless-cni-infra/2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a/attached /run/rootless-cni-infra/2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a/attached-args
+ pid=
+ '[' -f /run/rootless-cni-infra/2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a/pid ]
+ pid=19293
+ wait_unshare_net 19293
+ pid=19293
+ i=0
+ :
+ unshare -n sleep infinity
+ readlink /proc/self/ns/net
+ readlink /proc/19293/ns/net
+ '[' 'net:[4026532484]' '!=' 'net:[4026533434]' ]
+ break
+ echo 19293
+ nsenter -t 19293 -n ip link set lo up
+ CNI_ARGS='IgnoreUnknown=1;K8S_POD_NAME=wonderful_edison'
+ '['  ]
+ '['  ]
+ '['  ]
+ find /run/rootless-cni-infra/2fceea5b8c3fd6c890dbf14f98e57e557a9f38d77c50ac271280cd3ce524793a/attached -type f
+ wc -l
+ nwcount=0
+ CNI_IFNAME=eth0
+ export CNI_ARGS CNI_IFNAME CAP_ARGS
+ cnitool add foo /proc/19293/ns/net
exit status 3
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.0
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Git Commit:   5b2585f5e91ca148f068cefa647c23f8b1ade622
Built:        Thu Feb 11 22:22:46 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical machine running Arch Linux

comments.body:
Can you paste your cni config. Does it fail for all containers/networks?
Can you run the podman command with `--log-level debug` and provide the output.
Also try to remove the rootless-cni-infra container.

> Also try to remove the rootless-cni-infra container.

Tried, but it doesn't help.

> Does it fail for all containers/networks?

All the ones I tried so far, created using `podman network create` (with and without `--subnet` flag)

> Can you paste your cni config.

```json
{
  "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
  "name": "foo",
  "plugins": [
    {
      "type": "bridge",
      "bridge": "cni-podman0",
      "isGateway": true,
      "ipMasq": true,
      "hairpinMode": true,
      "ipam": {
        "type": "host-local",
        "routes": [
          {
            "dst": "0.0.0.0/0"
          }
        ],
        "ranges": [
          [
            {
              "subnet": "10.88.2.0/24",
              "gateway": "10.88.2.1"
            }
          ]
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "portmap",
      "capabilities": {
        "portMappings": true
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "firewall",
      "backend": ""
    },
    {
      "type": "tuning"
    },
    {
      "type": "dnsname",
      "domainName": "dns.podman",
      "capabilities": {
        "aliases": true
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

> Can you run the podman command with --log-level debug and provide the output.

OK I have no idea what is causing this. One difference between your setup and mine is that I am using crun v0.17 instead of runc. Might be worth to try.

Tried with crun v0.17 with no luck. Do you know what `Exit status 3` means in `cnitool` ? I've looked at the source code (which I suppose is this https://github.com/containernetworking/cni/blob/master/cnitool/cnitool.go) but I couldn't figure it out.

@Luap99 took me a while to figure it out, but looks like Apparmor got in the middle of the dnsname plugin:

From within the `rootless-cni-infra` container:

```
~ # /usr/sbin/dnsmasq -u root --conf-file=/run/containers/cni/dnsname/test/dnsmasq.conf

dnsmasq: cannot read /run/containers/cni/dnsname/test/dnsmasq.conf: Permission denied
```

And in journalctl:

```
Feb 16 15:33:34 jason audit[139229]: AVC apparmor="DENIED" operation="open" profile="dnsmasq" name="/run/containers/cni/dnsname/test/dnsmasq.conf" pid=139229 comm="dnsmasq" requested_mask="r" denied_mask="r" fsuid=1000 ouid=1000
```

Solved by running this:

```
 sudo apparmor_parser -R /etc/apparmor.d/usr.sbin.dnsmasq
```

I'm closing the issue now. Thanks for your help!
@michelesr mind adding that to containers/dnsname ? there is a mini tutorial in there iirc.
